sharedEntities.description: Core loan data representing loan contracts with attributes like status, type, and sale information. Flows from ingestion to processing to analytics pipelines.
Agreement terms between CRB and partner institutions with effective dates and parameters, ingested and then processed for analytics.
Fee structures and collection records for loans that are processed in DAGs and transformed for reporting.
Interest accrual records and rate information for loans, calculated in DAGs and analyzed in datatools.
Marketplace Lender entity with configuration for data processing, used to track relationships between loans and lenders.
SOFR rate data fetched from Federal Reserve, ingested and then used in loan calculations and processing.

dataFlows.name: Loan Data Processing Pipeline
dataFlows.description: End-to-end pipeline that ingests loan data from external sources, processes it through various transformations, and prepares it for analytics
steps: Ingest raw loan data from external sources (cos-lending-selling-ingestion)
Store raw data in S3 and databases
Orchestrate transformation workflows via Airflow DAGs (cos-lending-selling-dags)
Generate analytics and reports for the lending and selling business

dataFlows.name: SOFR Rate Processing
dataFlows.description: Pipeline for ingesting, storing, and applying SOFR rates to loan calculations
steps: Fetch SOFR rates from Federal Reserve API (cos-lending-selling-ingestion)
Use rates in interest calculations and loan processing (cos-lending-selling-dags)

dataFlows.name: Servicing Data Workflow
dataFlows.description: Process for handling loan servicing information to track adjusted amounts and interest payments
steps: Ingest servicing data from external sources (cos-lending-selling-ingestion)
Process servicing data through DAGs for fee collection and interest accrual (cos-lending-selling-dags)

integrationPoints.description: Raw loan, contract, and SOFR data is ingested and stored in databases which the DAGs then read from to process
Processed loan data flows from DAG processing to data tools for transformation and analytics via shared databases
Raw data is stored in S3 buckets after ingestion for downstream processing
Transformed data and analytics results are stored in S3 for reporting and access
Bidirectional data flow between DAGs and the Vampire system for loan processing

patterns.description: Classic ETL pattern used throughout the cluster to ingest, process, and prepare data for analytics
Airflow DAGs used to coordinate and schedule data processing workflows
Tracking timestamps of data ingestion per MPL to identify and process only changed data
Publishing business metrics to CloudWatch for monitoring operational performance
Using dbt to model and transform data for analytics purposes

ambiguities: The exact schema mapping between the different loan representations across the three repositories is undefined
The specific business rules that govern the loan selling process in cos-lending-selling-dags are not clear
The criteria for loan seasoning in cos-lending-selling-ingestion are not explicitly defined
The mechanism for reconciliation between servicing data and loan data in cos-lending-selling-ingestion is unclear
The business logic for fee calculation in cos-lending-selling-dags is not fully specified
The exact nature of the Vampire system and its data exchange patterns with cos-lending-selling-dags is ambiguous
